content:
Does anyone know if rustc might widen the alignment of a repr(C) struct , or a repr(C) struct in a repr(transparent) struct? @Gary Guo

The question came up here.

Basically in C alignment is set to the size of a struct to ensure array stride when the struct is packed into a section by the linker.

Gary Guo said:

No

As in "No it does not widen alignment ever"?
The ABI meaning of #[repr(C)] and #[repr(transparent)] is specified and can't change
But supposedly in C, the compiler can widen the alignment of a struct for performance reasons. If repr(C) is supposed to mimic C, then would it not do the same widening?
In C land they disable this by annotating with attribute __aligned(__alignof__(struct kernel_param)).
#[repr(C)] is a misnomer actually, it means organise fields linearly while respecting alignment
